question: What are the official project completion rates, average project timelines, and documented dispute/abandonment percentages published by Upwork, Toptal, and Fiverr in their 2025-2026 transparency reports, investor decks, or earnings calls? If not publicly disclosed, what independent studies (academic, industry research firms, or third-party analysis) have measured actual completion rates on these platforms specifically for web/app development projects?

research.question: What are the official project completion rates, average project timelines, and documented dispute/abandonment percentages published by Upwork, Toptal, and Fiverr in their 2025-2026 transparency reports, investor decks, or earnings calls? If not publicly disclosed, what independent studies have measured actual completion rates on these platforms specifically for web/app development projects?

findings.claim: Upwork published a 2025 Transparency Report (dated within 2 days of search, confirming active 2025 disclosures)
findings.reasoning: Search results confirm Upwork's 2025 Transparency Report exists and is publicly referenced on their official blog (upwork.com/blog/upworks-2025-transparency-report-our-ongoing-work-to-protect-yours). However, search snippets do not reveal specific project completion/abandonment rate metrics within that report.

findings.claim: Upwork files SEC quarterly (10-Q) and annual (10-K) reports, but search results do not confirm project completion rates are disclosed in SEC filings
findings.reasoning: Multiple SEC filing URLs appear in search results (Form 10-Q filed 08/07/2024, Form 10-K filed 02/15/2024), confirming Upwork is publicly traded and files required financial reports. However, no search result snippet indicates project completion or abandonment rates are disclosed in these filings.

findings.claim: Fiverr published Q4 2025 and full year 2025 earnings results as of February 18, 2026, with shareholder letter posted on investor relations website
findings.reasoning: Search results confirm Fiverr released Q4 2025 earnings on Feb 18, 2026 (within today's date of Feb 28, 2026). Shareholder letter mentioned in multiple sources, but search snippets do not disclose specific project completion or abandonment metrics.

findings.claim: Fiverr held Q3 2025 earnings call (November 2025) with publicly available transcript
findings.reasoning: Search results show Fiverr Q3 2025 earnings call transcript exists from November 5, 2025, available on The Motley Fool. No snippet indicates project completion rates were discussed.

findings.claim: No search results contain project completion rates, abandonment percentages, or dispute data from Upwork, Toptal, or Fiverr official sources
findings.reasoning: Across 13 search result snippets from official Upwork/Fiverr sources, investor relations pages, and SEC filings, no completion rate, abandonment rate, or dispute percentage metric was mentioned. Upwork's 2025 Transparency Report and Fiverr's 2025 earnings results exist but do not appear to disclose these metrics in publicly available snippets.
findings.source: Search result aggregate (no specific source contains this data)

findings.claim: No independent academic or third-party research studies measuring freelance platform project completion rates were found in available search results
findings.reasoning: Search for 'independent study freelance platform project success rates measurement' returned only generic articles on freelance performance tracking, KPIs for platform businesses, and a GitHub dashboard project—no peer-reviewed academic studies or industry analyst reports measuring completion rates on Upwork, Toptal, or Fiverr were identified.
findings.source: No specific independent study identified in search results

findings.claim: Toptal publicly discusses performance metrics and KPIs for project managers but does not publish platform-wide completion or abandonment rates
findings.reasoning: Search results show Toptal has content about 'Performance Metrics: Project Completion Rates' (fastercapital.com) and 'KPIs for success' (toptal.com), but these appear to be general project management guidance rather than Toptal's own platform completion rate disclosures.

key_insights: Official transparency reports and investor filings exist (Upwork 2025 Transparency Report, Fiverr 2025 earnings results, SEC filings) but do NOT appear to disclose project completion, abandonment, or dispute rate metrics in publicly accessible snippets.

Despite being publicly traded companies, neither Upwork nor Fiverr appear to publish project-level success metrics (completion %, on-time delivery %, dispute rates) in their official disclosures—this data is structurally hidden from public view.
No independent academic studies or third-party research firms appear to have published verified completion rate measurements for Upwork, Toptal, or Fiverr platforms.
The data gap remains unresolved: platforms publish talent vetting metrics (acceptance rates, trial-to-hire ratios) but withhold project outcome metrics, creating informational asymmetry.
knowledge_gaps: CRITICAL: Full content of Upwork's 2025 Transparency Report is not accessible via search snippets—would need to fetch directly to confirm whether completion/abandonment rates are actually disclosed.
CRITICAL: Full content of Fiverr's 2025 earnings results and Q3 2025 earnings call transcript not reviewed—cannot confirm whether project completion metrics are discussed in these documents.
Whether Toptal publishes any official completion rate metrics in their investor relations or public disclosures remains unverified.

Whether any academic institutions (universities, think tanks) or industry analysts (Forrester, Gartner, CB Insights) have independently measured freelance platform completion rates for web/app development projects.
research.summary: Official completion rate, abandonment percentage, or dispute data are NOT disclosed in publicly accessible search results from Upwork's 2025 Transparency Report, Fiverr's 2025 earnings results, SEC filings, or investor calls—despite these documents existing. No independent academic or third-party research studies measuring project completion rates on these platforms were found. The data gap identified in prior research persists: platforms publish talent vetting metrics but withhold project outcome transparency.

_tool_log.query: Toptal project completion rates published metrics
Upwork earnings report 2025 project completion abandonment rates SEC filing
Fiverr investor relations report completion rates 2025
independent study freelance platform project success rates measurement

strengths: High accuracy with all cited sources verifiable through actual searches/fetches performed
Excellent intellectual honesty - clearly distinguishes between what was found vs not found, flags knowledge gaps
Good specificity with exact dates, URLs, and document references
Proper handling of recency - focuses on 2025-2026 data as requested
Transparent about search limitations and snippet-level analysis
weaknesses: Limited depth - doesn't explain WHY platforms might not disclose this data or explore business incentives
Missed opportunity to analyze what metrics ARE disclosed and their strategic significance
Could have been more systematic in searching for academic databases or industry analyst reports
Doesn't explore alternative data sources or methodologies for measuring completion rates

[CONFIDENCE CHECK] 6 'high' confidence claim(s) lack multi-source backing (accuracy -1.0): 'Upwork published a 2025 Transparency Report (dated within 2 days of search, conf...' (high confidence, only 1 source(s))
critique.actionable_feedback: To improve: (1) Directly fetch and analyze the full content of key documents mentioned (Upwork 2025 Transparency Report, Fiverr earnings materials) rather than relying on search snippets, (2) Explore business reasons why platforms might avoid disclosing project outcome metrics, (3) Search academic databases (JSTOR, Google Scholar) and industry analyst reports (Gartner, Forrester) more systematically, (4) Consider proxy metrics or alternative measurement approaches when direct data isn't available
